data.title: China Everbright Greentech Sets 2026 AGM, Proposes Dividend and Share Issue Mandate
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from China Everbright Greentech Ltd. ( (HK:1257) ).

China Everbright Greentech Limited has called its annual general meeting for 15 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK2.8 cents per share. The meeting will also consider the re-election of several executive and independent non-executive directors, the re-appointment of KPMG as auditor, and a refreshed general mandate authorizing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, potentially providing flexibility for future capital-raising or strategic transactions while reaffirming existing governance and board oversight structures.

These resolutions, including the continuation of long-serving independent directors, indicate an emphasis on board stability and continuity as the company navigates its operating environment. The proposed share issuance mandate, subject to shareholder approval, would give management additional financial flexibility within regulatory limits, which could influence future funding strategies and capital structure decisions for stakeholders.

More about China Everbright Greentech Ltd.

China Everbright Greentech Limited is a Hong Kong–listed environmental protection and renewable energy company focused on greentech solutions in Mainland China and the region. It operates in areas such as waste-to-energy, biomass, hazardous waste treatment and other environmental services, targeting sustainable infrastructure and green development markets.

data.description:

Archosaur Games Inc. ( (HK:9990) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Archosaur Games Inc. has called its annual general meeting for 26 June 2026 in Beijing, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the related directors’ and auditor’s reports. Investors will also vote on the re-election of non-executive and independent non-executive directors, the re-appointment of PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, and authorisation for the board to set remuneration for directors and the auditor.

The AGM agenda further seeks shareholder approval for a general mandate allowing the board to issue, allot and deal in additional shares up to 20% of the company’s existing issued share capital, excluding treasury shares and subject to certain exceptions. If granted, this mandate would give Archosaur Games added flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions in the future, potentially supporting growth initiatives while diluting existing holdings only within a predefined limit.

More about Archosaur Games Inc.

Archosaur Games Inc. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the video game and interactive entertainment industry. The group develops and publishes games, primarily targeting players in mainland China and potentially other global markets, and is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

data.title: Dalipal Taps KUKA AI Robotics to Power Saudi Intelligent Manufacturing Base
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dalipal Holdings Limited ( (HK:1921) ) has issued an announcement.

Dalipal Holdings, through its subsidiary Dalipal International, has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with KUKA Robotics to introduce AI-enabled intelligent robotics and automation at its planned high-end energy equipment manufacturing base in Saudi Arabia. The partnership will use the Saudi Dammam SPARK Industrial Park project as a demonstration site, integrating AI industrial robots, machine vision, and adaptive control to boost machining accuracy, production speed, and product quality.

KUKA will also deploy automated guided vehicles, intelligent logistics, and process-optimisation platforms to enable largely unmanned, digitally managed production from raw materials to finished goods, aiming to reduce downtime and operating costs. The cooperation includes technology transfer, AI training, and jointly set performance metrics, positioning Dalipal to strengthen its competitive edge, support its localisation and supplier ambitions in the Middle East, and build a sustainable AI talent base for long-term growth.

More about Dalipal Holdings Limited

Dalipal Holdings Limited is a manufacturer of high-end energy equipment, with a growing focus on intelligent and digitalised production for international markets. Through its subsidiary Dalipal International For Industry, the Group is building an intelligent manufacturing base in Saudi Arabia’s Dammam SPARK Industrial Park to serve core energy markets in the Middle East.

data.title: Beijing Gas Blue Sky Secures HK$400 Million Revolving Loan Tied to State Ownership
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Beijing Gas Blue Sky Holdings Ltd ( (HK:6828) ) has issued an announcement.

Beijing Gas Blue Sky Holdings Limited has accepted a HK$400 million uncommitted revolving loan facility from a bank, with each drawdown carrying a tenor of up to six months and an initial overall maturity of one year, extendable at the bank’s discretion for another year. The financing arrangement enhances the company’s liquidity flexibility but embeds stringent change-of-control provisions that allow the lender to cancel the facility and demand immediate repayment if the Beijing state-controlled ownership chain through Beijing Enterprises Group, Beijing Gas Group and ultimately the company is disrupted, signaling the importance of continued state-backed control to its capital access and risk profile.

Under the terms of the facility, any loss of status by the Beijing municipal SASAC as the single largest shareholder of Beijing Enterprises Group, or any fall of Beijing Enterprises Group’s or Beijing Gas Group’s stake below 51% in their respective subsidiaries, would constitute a change of control. Upon such an event, the bank would not be obliged to make further loans and could, with 14 days’ notice, cancel the facility and accelerate all outstanding amounts, and the company has committed to ongoing disclosure of these covenants in its interim and annual reports as long as these obligations remain in force.

More about Beijing Gas Blue Sky Holdings Ltd

Beijing Gas Blue Sky Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed energy company involved in the natural gas sector under the wider Beijing municipal state-owned enterprise system. It forms part of a shareholding chain led by Beijing Gas Group Co., Ltd. and ultimately overseen by Beijing Enterprises Group Company Limited, which is supervised by the State-owned Assets Supervision and Administration Commission of the Beijing municipal government.

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited ( (HK:0818) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited has launched Phase 3 of its Suixin Cloud Chain-E Credit Supply Chain Financial Asset-backed Special Scheme in mainland China, issuing RMB169 million of asset-backed securities backed by factoring loan receivables. The ABS are split into a AAAsf-rated priority tranche of RMB143 million, listed for trading on the Shanghai Stock Exchange and placed with qualified institutional investors, and a subordinated tranche of RMB26 million subscribed by Ronghui Zhida Factoring or its affiliates, with underlying assets remaining consolidated on the group’s balance sheet.

Proceeds from the transaction will primarily support general working capital for the group’s fintech services business, a move the board says will diversify funding channels and lower capital costs while optimising the company’s financing structure. Directors emphasise that the ABS issuance should not have a material adverse financial impact on the group or shareholders’ interests and characterise the deal as non-price-sensitive, positioning it as a routine funding exercise within the company’s broader capital management strategy.

Hi Sun Technology (China) Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated investment holding company whose subsidiaries focus on payment and digital services, fintech services, platform operation solutions and financial solutions in mainland China. The group’s fintech segment leverages supply chain finance and asset securitisation to support its working capital and operational growth needs.

data.title: Taihe Rural Micro-finance Faces Extended Trading Halt Amid Governance Gaps
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Yangzhou Guangling District Taihe Rural Micro-finance Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1915) ) has provided an announcement.

Yangzhou Guangling District Taihe Rural Micro-finance Company Limited has received additional resumption guidance from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange after recent resignations left it in breach of multiple corporate governance rules. The company currently falls short of requirements on the number and board representation of independent non-executive directors, as well as proper composition of its audit, remuneration and nomination committees.

As a result, trading in the company’s shares, which has been suspended since April 1, 2025 due to delayed financial reporting and other compliance issues, will remain halted until it fully satisfies all resumption conditions. The exchange has emphasized that the company must devise a credible action plan to restore full compliance, and investors are urged to exercise caution given the ongoing uncertainty around governance and the timing of trading resumption.

More about Yangzhou Guangling District Taihe Rural Micro-finance Company Limited Class H

Yangzhou Guangling District Taihe Rural Micro-finance Company Limited is a mainland China-based rural microfinance lender listed in Hong Kong. The group focuses on providing small-scale loan financing services to customers in Yangzhou’s Guangling District and surrounding rural areas, operating under a regulated framework set by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and mainland financial authorities.

Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Company., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2517) ) has provided an announcement.

Guoquan Food (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. plans to seek shareholder approval at its 2025 annual general meeting for a mandate allowing the board to repurchase up to 10% of its H shares, excluding treasury stock, through 2026. Under this proposed mandate, the board has resolved to implement an H share repurchase plan of up to HK$200 million on the open market, with prices capped at no more than 5% above the average closing price over the preceding five trading days.

The company may cancel repurchased shares or hold them as treasury stock and will continue buybacks under its existing 2024 mandate until the 2025 meeting. Management says the current share price undervalues the firm’s intrinsic worth and prospects, viewing the repurchase as a way to signal confidence, enhance shareholder value, and support its market positioning, while pledging compliance with Hong Kong listing, takeover, and public float rules and warning that execution will depend on market conditions and board discretion.

data.title: Wasion Sets 2026 AGM to Approve Dividend, Board Changes and Capital Mandates
data.description:

Wasion Holdings Limited ( (HK:3393) ) just unveiled an update.

Wasion Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 15 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the directors’ report, audited financial statements and auditor’s report for the year ended 31 December 2025. The board is also proposing a final dividend of HK$0.48 per share, several director re-elections and a new independent non-executive appointment, along with approval to set director and auditor remuneration.

Shareholders will be asked to renew mandates allowing the board to buy back up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital and to issue new shares or resell treasury shares up to 20% of issued capital, excluding rights issues and certain share schemes. These authorisations, if passed, would give the board continued flexibility in capital management and equity issuance, potentially affecting share liquidity, ownership structure and future financing options for investors.

More about Wasion Holdings Limited

Wasion Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and trades under stock code 3393. The group operates in the broader industrial and technology sector, and its governance structure features a mix of executive and independent non-executive directors overseeing its corporate and financial affairs.

data.description:

Bank of China ( (HK:3988) ) has issued an update.

Bank of China has disclosed the poll results of its first extraordinary general meeting of 2026, held on April 22 in Beijing, with both on-site and online participation. The meeting attracted 3,623 shareholders and proxies representing about 82.15% of the bank’s voting share capital, indicating robust investor engagement and a high level of shareholder turnout.

Shareholders approved the reappointment of Chairman Ge Haijiao as an executive director, with over 99% of votes cast in favour and minimal opposition or abstentions. The result reinforces leadership continuity at the state-owned lender and suggests strong investor support for the current governance and strategic direction at a time when Chinese banks are navigating regulatory, economic, and market headwinds.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:3988) stock is a Buy with a HK$5.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bank of China stock, see the HK:3988 Stock Forecast page.

More about Bank of China

Bank of China Limited is one of China’s largest state-owned commercial banks, operating across corporate and retail banking, financial markets, and international banking services. Listed in Hong Kong and Shanghai, it serves both domestic and overseas clients, with a substantial free-float of A shares and H shares that underscores its systemic importance in China’s financial sector and global markets.
